clinical_coherence.notes: 58F Hispanic with HTN, DM2, hyperlipidemia, OSA, CKD3a, no known AF. Wake-up stroke with subtle right neglect and dysarthria. Vitals appropriate. Neuro exam with line bisection deviation and visual extinction localizing to left parietal lobe. Well-constructed wake-up stroke presentation.

tool_output_consistency.notes: Generally concordant. MRI shows DWI+/FLAIR+ in left MCA territory. ECG shows sinus rhythm with LAE. CTA shows moderate left ICA stenosis (60-69%) with high-risk plaque. Holter reveals paroxysmal AF. Minor issue: homocysteine value discrepancy between interpretation text (16.8) and actual value field (15.6), though both are mildly elevated.

ground_truth_quality.notes: Excellent. Correctly identifies mixed etiology. Critical actions include DWI-FLAIR mismatch assessment, vascular imaging for LAA disease, prolonged monitoring for paroxysmal AF, and vascular surgery consultation for symptomatic moderate stenosis with high-risk plaque. Well-calibrated differential.

difficulty_calibration.notes: Appropriately moderate. Very similar structure to M01 (wake-up stroke, sinus rhythm masking paroxysmal AF, LAE as clue, mixed mechanism). The addition of symptomatic carotid stenosis requiring surgical consideration adds a management dimension. Slightly less novel than M01 given the repetitive structure.

demographic_plausibility.notes: 58F Hispanic with metabolic syndrome cluster is plausible. No known AF history adds realism. PMH again follows the amlodipine/losartan/metformin/glipizide pattern but is more differentiated with no prior cardiac history.

educational_value.notes: Good case reinforcing wake-up stroke and occult AF concepts from M01. The addition of symptomatic carotid stenosis with high-risk plaque morphology and surgical consideration adds educational depth. However, significant overlap with M01 in core teaching points.

issues_found: Homocysteine value discrepancy between interpretation text (16.8) and data field (15.6)
